methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Neuromeka is a publicly listed robotics and manufacturing company on KOSDAQ (ticker: 277810.KQ) that has raised $36.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of nine peers: one public company and eight private robotics firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Standard peer-median triangulation is not applicable here due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the Robotics & Manufacturing sector. While eleven same-sector peers exist in the broader universe, filtering by stage and valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints in any single bucket, rendering a narrow median approach unreliable. An all-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias given the heterogeneity of robotics subsegments and commercialization timelines.

Without viable median or mean multiples derived from sufficiently sized cohorts, this analysis cannot support a specific valuation range or methodology-based recommendation. The single public peer and fragmented private financing landscape limit triangulation precision. Valuation assessment would require either (1) direct revenue or EBITDA comparables if Neuromeka's financials are disclosed, (2) expansion of the comp set to adjacent automation or hardware verticals, or (3) transaction-based precedent data from recent robotics M&A. Key uncertainties include Neuromeka's revenue scale, gross margins, and growth trajectory relative to stated peers.
